article_meta.title: — News —Study Shows Pandemic Saw Rise In Firearm Injuries Among KidsBy Charlotte Pence Bond•Dec 20, 2022 DailyWire.com•F

reasoning: The model has several issues: (1) It uses bias type labels not from the defined list ('Framing Bias', 'Emphasis Bias', 'Omission Bias', 'Contextual Bias' instead of the valid types like 'Slant', 'Bias by Omission', 'Word Choice', etc.). (2) One flagged item is attributed WSJ language ('Researchers said psychological stress...' is noted as 'WSJ notes'). (3) The third item flags text that isn't actually in the article - it's the model's own summary of what's missing, which is a valid concept (Bias by Omission) but poorly executed since the 'biasedText' field contains invented text. (4) However, some items are defensible: the 'disastrous effect' framing is genuinely the author's loaded language, and the omission of gun policy discussion is a legitimate observation. The fourth item about pandemic policies is from the Kaiser Family Foundation attribution, making it borderline.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 2 flags text attributed to WSJ ('WSJ notes') which is an attribution violation. Item 4 references Kaiser Family Foundation content. Items 1 and 3 are from author prose or structural choices respectively, so those are fine. Mixed compliance.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Item 1 ('disastrous effect') is a defensible catch of author-level loaded language. Item 3 (omission of gun policy) is a defensible structural observation though the biasedText is fabricated. Item 2 is attributed to WSJ. Item 4 is borderline - it references KFF content but the author chose to include it. Overall moderate false positive issues.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model did catch the key 'disastrous' framing and the omission angle. It could have more precisely flagged 'another disastrous effect' as Word Choice or Subjective Qualifying Adjectives. The overall slant of the article toward pandemic-as-cause framing was noted. Reasonably complete coverage.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: None of the bias type labels used ('Framing Bias', 'Emphasis Bias', 'Omission Bias', 'Contextual Bias') are from the defined list of valid types. 'Omission Bias' is close to 'Bias by Omission' but not exact. 'Framing Bias' could map to 'Spin' or 'Slant'. This is a systematic labeling failure.
